what_it_does: Claude Code per-project settings: sets worktree.bgIsolation to 'none' and registers a single SessionStart hook that runs the bash script ops/hooks/cc_registry_freshness_check.sh at the start of each Claude Code session.
observations: worktree.bgIsolation is 'none' — background isolation disabled for worktrees.
The one hook fires on SessionStart and shells out to /home/tim/company/ops/hooks/cc_registry_freshness_check.sh via an absolute path.
standouts: A SessionStart hook wired to a repo-local registry-freshness check — the repo bootstraps an orientation/staleness check on every session.
conventions: Uses an absolute path to the hook script rather than a relative or $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR reference.
Minimal config — only the keys actually in use are present.
concerns: Hardcoded absolute path /home/tim/company/... makes this non-portable to any other checkout location.
notes: Project Claude Code config; its only active behavior is a SessionStart hook that runs a registry-freshness bash check.
questions: What does cc_registry_freshness_check.sh check and how does it surface staleness?
Why is bgIsolation explicitly set to 'none' rather than left default?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (config file, no stated purpose); content is internally consistent.
intention_signals: Ensure every Claude Code session begins by verifying the live registry/self-description is fresh, embedding the repo's 'self-description is part of the change' discipline into the tooling itself.
novelty: Wiring a registry-freshness check into the SessionStart hook ties agent tooling to the repo's self-description discipline.
contribution: Contributes the concrete mechanism that auto-runs a registry-freshness check at session start, plus the worktree bgIsolation setting.
summary_for_embedding: Claude Code project settings.json that disables worktree background isolation and runs a registry-freshness bash hook on SessionStart.
intention_for_embedding: To make every Claude Code session start by checking the live registry/self-description for staleness.
